draft.introduction: The earliest unambiguous reference to bodily resurrection in the Hebrew Bible. Those who sleep in the dust shall awake, some to everlasting life and some to shame—a text that shaped all later resurrection theology.

translation.text: At that time shall arise Michael, the great prince who has charge of your people. And there shall be a time of trouble, such as never has been. And many of those who sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt. And those who are wise shall shine like the brightness of the sky above.
translation.original_text: καὶ πολλοὶ τῶν καθευδόντων ἐν πλάτει γῆς ἀναστήσονται

translator_notes.note: “Those who sleep in the dust” (τῶν καθευδόντων) uses sleep as a euphemism for death, a convention Paul adopts in 1 Thessalonians 4:13-15. The “shining like stars” imagery influenced the transfiguration accounts and Paul’s resurrection body theology in 1 Corinthians 15.
